default_creds.pass: (no password - hardcoded unsigned JWT via DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE)
default_creds.context: Default infra-as-code/docker-compose.yml bakes a fixed JWT (sub=1234567890, name=John Doe, email=john@acme.org) into the app service env; every request to the quickstart deployment is authenticated as this identity with no login
Postgres/pgvector container in default compose; also application_user:testpassword in APP_DATABASE_URL/DATABASE_URL

install_tell: curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bionic-gpt/bionic-gpt/main/infra-as-code/docker-compose.yml && docker compose up   # web console -> http://localhost:3000 (app internal 7703); k8s/helm path also documented for production with oauth2-proxy

response_markers: OpenAI-compatible JSON model list returned without an Authorization header (proves DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE / no fronting oauth2-proxy)
axum server response on internal port 7703 mapped to 3000
console / assistants / datasets routes load unauthenticated
active_probe.false_positive_check: "Bionic", "chat", and "AI" collide with thousands of unrelated hosts - do NOT fingerprint on those alone. Discriminator: the Bionic app is a Rust/axum server that on a default quickstart answers /v1/chat/completions and /v1/models OpenAI-style on port 3000 (->7703) WITHOUT any Authorization header because DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE is set and no oauth2-proxy fronts it. To avoid the ~50% false positive vs a generic OpenAI-compatible gateway (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama, LocalAI): confirm BOTH (a) the Bionic web console HTML (unique routes /console, /datasets, /assistants, audit_trail, team-based RAG UI) AND (b) the OpenAI-compatible /v1 surface answering unauth on the SAME host. A host serving only /v1 is a generic LLM proxy, not Bionic. A production Bionic behind oauth2-proxy/barricade returns 302->IdP on /console and is NOT the open-default case - so an unauth 200 on /console with the Bionic asset bundle is the load-bearing confirmation.

deployment_tells: Published port 3000 mapped to internal axum port 7703 (the app default PORT in config.rs is 7703)
Docker images ghcr.io/bionic-gpt/bionicgpt:1.12.x, bionicgpt-rag-engine, bionicgpt-db-migrations, bionicgpt-postgres-mcp
Bundled ankane/pgvector Postgres with POSTGRES_PASSWORD=testpassword (compose-only tell)
kreuzberg doc-engine container (ghcr.io/kreuzberg-dev/kreuzberg) for document parsing/OCR
README architecture: Nginx Router -> OAuth2 Proxy -> Rust axum server -> Postgres/pgVector; production = Kubernetes (k3s/EKS/AKS/GKE) helm
Written in Rust (axum); 'Ultra Fast Rust based UI' marketing string
extra_hosts host.docker.internal:host-gateway (to reach a host Ollama)
pivot_paths: GET /v1/models then /v1/chat/completions unauth -> confirm open LLM relay and enumerate which provider/models are wired (read-only)
Browse /console, /assistants, /datasets, /documents unauth -> enumerate ingested RAG corpora and assistant system prompts (names ARE the finding; do not download corpora)
Read Models/Providers admin page -> upstream LLM provider API key presence (metadata-confirm, do not exfiltrate)
Read /audit_trail and team roster -> operator attribution + SSO domain (org identification)
If 5432 also exposed: bundled Postgres pgvector at postgres:testpassword -> schema-level read of conversation history + embeddings (enumerate tables, do not dump)
GET /history -> conversation history confirming live use vs honeypot
vulnerabilities: No published GHSA/CVE for bionic-gpt/bionic-gpt as of 2026-06-12 (security advisories page: 'There aren't any published security advisories'). Latest release v1.12.11 (2026-02-12).

Design exposure (not a CVE): crates/web-server/jwt.rs never verifies the JWT signature - it base64-decodes the payload and trusts sub/email; signature validation is wholly delegated to the upstream oauth2-proxy. If the proxy is removed/misconfigured, any attacker-crafted unsigned JWT or any X-Forwarded-User/X-Forwarded-Email header is accepted as identity.
Design exposure (not a CVE): DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE env var ships SET in the default quickstart compose, authenticating all traffic as a fixed admin identity with no login or rate limit.
Related upstream (only if oauth2-proxy IS deployed): CVE-2025-54576 oauth2-proxy auth bypass - scope-limited, applies to the fronting component not the Bionic app itself.
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: the quickstart artifact (docs "Docker Compose" page tells users to `curl -O .../infra-as-code/docker-compose.yml` then `docker compose up`). That default infra-as-code/docker-compose.yml file (1) carries the header comment "Do not expose this configuration to the internet... This is NOT a production configuration", (2) publishes the `app` service as `3000:7703`, (3) sets `DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE: eyJhbGci...` on the app service (a hardcoded unsigned JWT: sub=1234567890, name="John Doe", email=john@acme.org), and (4) ships NO oauth2-proxy / barricade / keycloak / nginx-auth service. The auth model lives entirely in crates/web-server/jwt.rs: the Jwt FromRequestParts extractor does `if let Ok(override_token) = std::env::var("DANGER_JWT_OVERRIDE") { Some(override_token) } else { ...X-Forwarded-Access-Token header... }`. When the override is set EVERY request is authenticated as that identity, and even on the header path the token signature is NEVER verified (it base64-decodes the middle segment and reads sub/email out of the JSON). Real authentication is delegated to an upstream oauth2-proxy/barricade that injects X-Forwarded-User / X-Forwarded-Email (see config.rs `enable_barricade = env::var("ENABLE_BARRICADE").is_ok()` and README architecture diagram User -> Nginx -> OAuth2 Proxy -> Rust Server). Net: a user who runs the documented quickstart and exposes port 3000 gets a fully unauthenticated UI+API where every visitor is the baked-in admin "John Doe" - auth EXISTS (oauth2-proxy) but is OFF in the shipped default and must be added for production.
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM provider API keys (OpenAI / Anthropic / Ollama / custom provider creds configured in the Models/Providers admin UI - readable+editable as the baked-in admin)
Ingested RAG documents and datasets (enterprise PDF/CSV/HTML/PPTX uploaded to Assistants - often internal/PII-laden), stored as chunks+embeddings in Postgres pgvector
Assistant / Agentic-RAG pipeline definitions and system prompts (reveal business logic and data sources)
Full conversation history (README: 'All questions and responses are recording and available in the Postgres database')
Raw vector store contents (pgvector embeddings + source text in the bundled Postgres, password testpassword)
API keys minted for assistants (the OpenAI-compatible Assistants API keys) and MCP API keys
Audit trail / team membership / SSO-mapped user roster
Bundled Postgres at testpassword reachable if 5432 also exposed - direct DB read of everything above
